Article Title: Jeff Bezos: As Jeff Bezos 'celebrates' post on first-ever purchase made on Amazon, Elon Musk replies, says: It was start of ...
Article Text:
Why Jeff Bezos chose to sell books on Amazon





Jon Erlichman, BNN Bloomberg contributor and the host of Ticker Take, a YouTube channel dedicated to investing and financial literacy recently shared photos of Amazon ’s first non-employee purchase. The X post included original packing slip and Douglas Hofstadter's "Fluid Concepts and Creative Analogies," a book exploring AI and computer models of thought. “On this day in 1995: the first item was purchased on Amazon. It was a book on artificial intelligence,” he captioned the post. Amazon founder and former CEO Jeff Bezos shared the post on this timeline with a smiling face emoji.The post caught the attention of Elon Musk who replied writing: “It was the start of something great”.For those unaware, Amazon was founded by Jeff Bezos on July 5, 1994 in Bellevue, Washington. Originally named ‘Cadabra’, it then operated as an online bookstore out of Bezos’ garage before expanding into an online commerce platform. Visitors to the website were then greeted with a message “Welcome to Amazon.com Books!" and the promise of being able to order from "one million titles".During the early days, Jeff Bezos ran the company along with his ex-wife MacKenzie Scott and seven other employees. Amazon celebrated its 30th anniversary last year. In a 1997 interview, Bezos revealed that he chose to sell books because there were more varieties of books than any other type of product.At that time, Bezos estimated there were about 3 million books in print, about half of which were in English."When you have that many items, you could literally build a store online that couldn't exist any other way," Bezos then said.Since then, the company has expanded to become one of the world’s largest online retailers, expanding businesses into groceries, web services, and entertainment. Amazon now has a market cap of 2.25 trillion.
Article Summary: Jon erlichman, bnn bloomberg contributor and the host of ticker take, a youtube channel dedicated to investing and financial literacy recently shared photos of amazon’s first non-employee purchase. The x post included original packing slip and douglas hofstadter's "fluid concepts and creative analogies," a book exploring ai and computer models of thought.

Article Title: NASA releases Artemis II mission images taken on iPhone 17 Pro Max
Article Text:
Image courtesy: NASA

How iPhones made it onto NASA’s Artemis II mission







When NASA's Artemis II spacecraft lifted off, it carried four astronauts, decades of engineering ambition and an extraordinary arsenal of cameras, smartphones and communications technology to document humanity’s return to lunar orbit in detail. NASA approved the use of personal smartphones aboard Artemis II, making it one of the first missions where astronauts have brought their own handsets into space. The astronauts onboard carried four units of iPhone 17 Pro Max.Now NASA has released a series of striking images captured on the iPhone 17 Pro Max during the Artemis II mission, marking the first time consumer smartphones have been officially used to document a crewed lunar mission. The photos, taken on April 2, 2026, show astronauts Reid Wiseman and Christina Koch aboard the Orion spacecraft, gazing back at Earth through its cabin windows.“NASA astronaut and Artemis II mission specialist Christina Koch peers out of one of the Orion spacecraft's main cabin windows, looking back at Earth, as the crew travels towards the Moon,” wrote NASA while sharing the image on Flickr.For the first time, astronauts aboard Artemis II are carrying personal smartphones—specifically four units of the iPhone 17 Pro Max.It sounds like a small detail, but getting those devices approved for spaceflight was anything but straightforward.The clearance process followed a strict four-phase protocol. First, the hardware is introduced to a safety panel. Then comes hazard identification—everything from moving parts to materials like glass that could shatter in microgravity. The third phase outlines mitigation strategies, and the final stage validates that those fixes actually work.Durability was a key factor. The iPhone 17 Pro Max uses Ceramic Shield 2, which Apple claims is tougher than any smartphone glass. But NASA’s concerns went beyond just breakability. Devices behave differently in microgravity—heat dissipation, battery stability, and even how they’re stored all matter.While the iPhone has drawn attention, NASA continues to rely on its traditional imaging equipment for mission-critical documentation. The crew is also using Nikon D5, Nikon Z9, and GoPro HERO4 Black cameras to capture high-resolution scientific and operational imagery. The iPhones complement these tools by providing accessible, everyday snapshots from the astronauts’ point of view.
Article Summary: Nasa has released a series of striking images captured on the iphone 17 pro max during the artemis ii mission. The photos, taken on april 2, 2026, show astronauts reid wiseman and christina koch aboard the orion spacecraft, gazing back at earth through its cabin windows.

Article Title: Warren Buffett on Bill Gates: We've had great times together, but I think he ...
Article Text:
Warren Buffett

What Warren Buffett hasn’t spoken to Bill Gates recently



Warren Buffet on buying and selling Apple







Renewed attention around Microsoft co-founder Bill gets has brought his decades-long relationship with Berkshire Hathaway Chair Warren Buffet back into the spotlight. In a recent CNBC interview, Buffett was asked directly about where things stand today. Buffett offered a carefully measured answer, focusing only on his personal experience over the years. “We’ve had great times together,” he said. “But he’s treated me better than I think he’s probably treated anybody else.” He did not define the current state of the friendship, instead keeping his remarks confined to how Gates had treated him personally.Buffett acknowledged that he has not spoken to Gates since the release of files related to the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein . He explained his reasoning candidly: “For one thing, I don’t want to be under, my memory is no good anymore. I don’t want to be under oath in terms of trying to remember everything over 30 years or 20 years.” He emphasized that his involvement in related philanthropic efforts was limited to financial contributions, saying, “I didn’t have anything to do with it, except I put the money in.”Talking about stocks, Warren Buffett said that he sold Apple too soon and would buy more of it, though not in the current market. “I sold it too soon.But, I bought it even sooner, so,” said Buffet. As per the report, quoting InsiderScore, Apple remains Berkshire Hathaway’s largest holding even after the conglomerate trimmed its stake to $61.96 billion at the end of last year.World's no. 1 investor Buffett also downplayed the recent market volatility. According to him, the current conditions fall far short of past periods that created major buying opportunities. “Three times since I took over, for sure it’s gone down more than 50%. ... This is nothing to make you get excited,” Buffett said.Also called “Oracle of Omaha”, Buffet revealed that Berkshire purchased $17 billion worth of Treasury bills recently at the weekly auction. Berkshire reported more than $370 billion in cash equivalents on the books at year-end.
Article Summary: Warren buffett hasn’t spoken to bill gates since the release of files related to the late convicted sex offender jeffrey epstein. “we’ve had great times together,” he said, “but he’s treated me better than i think he's probably treated anybody else” buffett downplayed the recent market volatility.

Article Title: Megan Thee Stallion: Why Klay Thompson and Megan Thee Stallion look like endgame despite breakup rumors
Article Text:
Klay Thompson and Megan Thee Stallion. Image via: theestallion/ Instagram

Klay Thompson and Megan Thee Stallion aren’t just dating—they’re already in their endgame era



PEOPLE

If you look at social media right now, you might think Klay Thompson and Megan Thee Stallion are over. People are pointing out that they don’t follow each other on Instagram, and gossip blogs are claiming they’ve split up. But if you look closer at the actual facts, it’s clear that these two aren't just "dating" they are endgame.Nothing says "I’m in this forever" like how they celebrated their back-to-back birthdays in February 2026. Megan didn't just throw a party, she organized "Klay Day." She planned a private beach picnic followed by a massive surprise bash featuring a live performance by Bone Thugs-N-Harmony, Klay’s favorite group since he was a kid.Klay didn't hold back, gifting her a tropical getaway and a brand-new Bentley. These aren't just expensive gifts; they are "forever" investments in each other's happiness.Klay Thompson is famous for his love of the ocean, but he recently made it official in the most permanent way possible. He renamed his beloved boat the "SS Stallion." For a man whose boat is his sanctuary, naming it after Megan is a public vow of loyalty.Megan also dropped a major clue that most people missed. She posted a photo of two hands, hers and Klay’s forming a heart around a set of house keys.This wasn’t just a cute picture. It was a "soft launch" of their biggest step yet: They bought a mansion together.Even more telling? The families are involved. During Thanksgiving 2025, Megan reportedly cooked the entire dinner for Klay and his family. When the parents are at the table and the boat has your name on it, the "dating" phase is officially over.The biggest proof of their "Endgame" status happened just last week. On April 1, 2026, Klay was a "no-show" for the Mavericks' game against the Milwaukee Bucks. While the team called it "rest," insiders confirmed Klay flew to New York to be with Megan after she was hospitalized for exhaustion.In the NBA , missing a game for a "girlfriend" is unheard of. By choosing to be by her side during a health scare, Klay showed that Megan is his #1 priority, even over basketball.Megan recently toldthat she has "never dated somebody so kind" and that Klay makes her "genuinely happy." This isn't the loud, chaotic love we usually see in the news. It’s a quiet, supportive partnership.From the shared house keys to the SS Stallion, every gesture points to one thing: they aren't playing for the cameras. They are playing for keeps.
Article Summary: Klay thompson and megan thee stallion aren’t just dating—they’re already in their endgame era. They celebrated their back-to-back birthdays in february 2026. Klay didn't hold back, gifting her a tropical getaway and a brand-new bentley.

Article Title: Bryce Young: Bryce Young’s Carolina Panthers strongly connected to $19M Cleveland Browns veteran tight end
Article Text:
Bryce Young’s Carolina Panthers strongly connected to $19M Cleveland Browns veteran tight end (Image via Getty: Carolina Panthers logo and Cleveland Browns logo)

NFL Trade Rumors : Bryce Young’s Panthers and Lamar Jackson ’s Ravens tied to Browns TE David Njoku

No Carolina tight end has recorded at least 500 yards after Greg Olsen

Cleveland tight end may sign for much less than predicted by Spotrac

Cleveland Browns tight end David Njoku’s 2025 season was marred by injuries. Rookie Harold Fannin Jr. took his spot for rookie quarterback Shedeur Sanders. However, CBS Sports’ Garrett Podell reports that when Njoku is healthy, he is a valuable middle-of-the-field and red-zone playmaker. That’s exactly where the Carolina Panthers need help.The Browns’ TE could really utilize the middle of the field for young quarterback Bryce Young. Podell reports that Carolina needs more receiving threat than Tommy Tremble has shown on tape. Tremble amassed 27 receptions for 249 yards and two touchdowns in 17 games.PFSN analyst T.J. Randall projects the Panthers to pick Oregon Ducks TE, Kenyon Sadiq. In 2025, Sadiq recorded 51 catches for 560 yards and eight touchdowns. The 6-foot-3, 241-pounder could be the rookie TE, and Njoku could be the veteran presence for him [speculation].As opposed to Tremble, Njoku’s 2025 stats were: 33 receptions for 293 yards and four touchdowns in just 12 games. In 2024: 64 receptions for 505 yards and five touchdowns in just 11 games. When remarkably healthy and durable in 2023: 81 receptions for 882 yards and six touchdowns in 16 games.ESPN’s Bill Barnwell reported that injuries took up Njoku’s playing time in 2024 and 2025.A knee injury kept him away for 10 games. His route efficiency was severely affected: after averaging 11.3 yards per catch during his three-year peak, he fell to 8.2 yards per catch over the past two seasons.From 22 catches, he came down to just five in 2024 and 2025. In the past five seasons, he also recorded the highest drop rate among TEs in the NFL [200 targets]. This could be because of the Browns’ inconsistent quarterback situation. There has been no consistent signal-caller in Cleveland.Anyhow, Barnwell picked Baltimore for Njoku, but they signed veteran tight end Durham Smythe to take up Charlie Kolar’s spot. Isaiah Likely’s spot is still open. Kolar may take that spot. They are also linked to drafting Kenyon Sadiq, the same Oregon TE with whom the Panthers are linked.If that doesn’t happen, then maybe they could sign a budget-friendly veteran behind Mark Andrews. That budget-friendly veteran could be Njoku? Anyhow, coming back to Carolina.Panthers Wire’s Anthony Rizzuti suggests that even though Carolina seems comfortable with their current TE room, it could use an upgrade in their production department.Since 2019, they have not had a single TE to record at least 500 receiving yards in a season. Greg Olson was the last one to do that. And now, Olsen’s a lead television sports commentator and analyst for FOX Sports.Anyhow, David Njoku had two down years, but even in one of those, he recorded 64 receptions for 505 yards and five touchdowns in 11 games. When he is healthy, his average receiving yards is typically well above 500.As per Spotrac, the 29-year-old Miami alum is projected to sign a two-year, $19 million contract, which gives him a $10 million average annual income. According to Over The Cap, Carolina has a $2.2 million in cap space.The CBS Sports analyst reports that Njoku will turn 30 in 2026 and has been dealing with injuries, so both entities could make him a “cost-conscious target.”Sports Illustrated’s Zach Roberts reports that Spotrac’s prediction was at the beginning of free agency; now the market has cooled off, so the veteran could sign for much less than what was predicted for him.So, would the Panthers consider a Cleveland tight end? Only time will tell…
Article Summary: Bryce young’s carolina panthers strongly connected to $19m cleveland browns veteran tight end david njoku. No carolina tight end has recorded at least 500 receiving yards since greg olsen in 2019. The browns’ te could really utilize the middle of the field for young quarterback bryce young.

Article Title: Formula E: Big Time explains Formula E energy management perfectly, fans say “send it to F1 drivers”
Article Text:
Formula E (Image credit: Formula E)

Here’s how energy management is mastered in Formula E



Watch Something Weird is Happening with Electric Race Cars: Coding the Chaos Ep.1 Something Weird is Happening with Electric Race Cars: Coding the Chaos Ep.1

Formula E fans react to first episode of Coding the Chaos

Formula E is currently in its twelfth season, having successfully delivered a sustainable form of motor racing entertainment for fans. The championship has gained even more popularity since Formula One introduced hybrid powertrains this season, which have left many seasoned F1 drivers nervous and frustrated, turning the pinnacle of motorsport into what some see as a strange experiment. Now, Big Time’s Jeremiah Burton has hosted a six-episode series to educate fans on the concept of energy management in Formula E races, with inputs from drivers like Jake Dennis, Lucas di Grassi and Zane Maloney.The Big Time video of Episode 1 - Coding the Chaos - was shared by Formula E on social media. In the first episode, Burton breaks down energy management in the simplest way possible, helping fans understand one of the most crucial aspects of the sport.This is the first episode of a six-part series exploring the engineering behind Formula E. The video highlights how energy management during a race is not just about the driver’s racing skills, but also about the entire team’s strategy, which ultimately decides the result.Adding his insight, Sao Paulo E-Prix winner Jake Dennis explains in the video, “I just love the unknown of Formula E. I think that’s what is great about it.You can basically lead the whole race, think you are doing a great job, but then in the last two laps, you can run out of energy and end up finishing sixth or seventh.”Lucas di Grassi also adds, “People have to understand we are pushing 100 percent all the time. It’s just that what changes is sometimes you are allowed to use more or less energy to gain or defend a position, which means you are driving fast but also efficiently. And if you do that, for sure, you are going to have a good result in the race.”Fans loved the way Jeremiah Burton explained the concept of energy management. Reacting to the video, one fan commented on YouTube: “Someone should send this video to the Formula 1 drivers, it may help them out.”Another said: “I’ve been watching F1 for over 40 years now, but this year it has changed so much. I’ll start watching Formula E as well, it should help me understand what F1 is trying to do.” One more added: “One of the best things about racing is the tech and driver skill. It is crazy to see how far the teams in Formula E take all those factors! Love it.” Another comment read: “The best part is that drivers and teams do not always know exactly how much battery they have. The fact that there is a lot less data makes for quite exciting races.”Formula E will return with Round 7 of Season 12 in Berlin. The Berlin E-Prix is scheduled from 1-3 May.Also read: Exclusive interview: Edoardo Mortara shines for Mahindra Racing this season, says “important to keep your feet on the ground”
Article Summary: Formula e is currently in its twelfth season, having delivered a sustainable form of motor racing entertainment for fans. Big time’s jeremiah burton has hosted a six-episode series to educate fans on the concept of energy management in formula e races. The big time video of episode 1 - coding the chaos - was shared by formula e on social media.

Article Title: Klay Thompson: Did Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson delete each other from social media amid breakup rumors?
Article Text:
Did Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson delete each other from social media amid breakup rumors?(Image via Getty Images)

Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson never added each other on their respective social media accounts

Megan Thee Stallion opened up about love and Klay Thompson

Megan Thee Stallion, the popular Grammy winner, and Klay Thompson seem to be pretty serious about their high profile relationship as they flaunt it to the world.Over the last few months, both of them have gifted expensive things to each other, including a Bentley gifted by Klay Thompson on Megan Thee Stallion’s birthday. Amid this, there have been constant rumours that the two have removed each other from their respective social media accounts.In the last few months, Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson have constantly dealt with such fake rumors about them removing one another on their respective social media accounts.Interestingly, though the two have been together for close to a year, Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson never added each other on social media.Though this has led to many break up rumors, both of them have stayed away from such speculations about their high profile relationship.While the reason behind this move is not known, both Megan Thee Stallion and Klay Thompson continue to post their romantic pictures on their accounts.Just a few weeks ago, in a heartfelt conversation with PEOPLE, Megan Thee Stallion spoke about love as she said, “Well, I don't never want to tell [anybody] to just jump in a relationship just because everybody else got one, and I'm not going to tell you to just jump in a relationship because you have to…”The “Hiss” rapper addendum “I didn't even know I was going to be in my relationship, to be honest. I think that because finally I started being in a better mind space about myself and my life, and I had already been doing a lot of work to heal me…”The three time Grammy winner continued, “I had been going to therapy, I had a bunch of activities that I started doing for myself; maybe God just opened up that space for me to have somebody that loved me right."Over the last few months, Megan Thee Stallion has often admitted her love and admiration for Klay Thompson as she called him the “kindest person”.Amid this, there have also been strong engagement rumors but it seems the power couple are focused on their respective successful careers at the moment.
Article Summary: Megan thee stallion and klay thompson never added each other on social media. There have been rumours that the two have removed each other from their respective social media accounts. Both of them continue to post their romantic pictures on their accounts. Over the last few months, both of them have gifted expensive things to each other.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Elon Musk: “When something is important enough, you do it even if the odds are not in your favour.”
Article Text:
Quote of the day by Elon Musk

Quote of the day by Elon Musk



“When something is important enough, you do it even if the odds are not in your favour.”

Understanding the meaning of the quote by Elon Musk



Act instead of waiting : Waiting for the right time may slow things down.

: Waiting for the right time may slow things down. Decisions based on purpose : Even when things are unclear, it's worth it to work toward important goals.

: Even when things are unclear, it's worth it to work toward important goals. Taking risks: When you want to achieve big things, you often have to deal with uncertainty.

Who is Elon Musk and why his words matter



At one time, electric cars weren't very popular, but Tesla helped make them popular all over the world.

SpaceX worked on reusable rockets, which at first seemed like a crazy idea but later became a big step forward in space technology.

Why acting despite the odds matters in real life



Decisions about work

Starting a business

Learning and going to school

The role of risk in progress and innovation



Scientific experiments can fail many times before they get results.

People often resist new technologies before they are accepted.

There is a lot of uncertainty in big projects, especially when it comes to space exploration.

How this idea connects to everyday life



Talking up in serious situations: Even when you're not sure what to say

Trying new things, like applying for a job or learning a new skill

Continuing to work hard after setbacks: Even though things have gone wrong in the past

Balancing risk with responsibility



Assessing risks: Knowing what might happen

Getting ready for problems: Making plans for different situations

Making choices based on facts: Making good use of the information you have

Other famous quotes by Elon Musk



“Failure is an option here. If things are not failing, you are not innovating enough.”

“Persistence is very important. You should not give up unless you are forced to give up.”

“Some people don’t like change, but you need to embrace change if the alternative is disaster.”

“I think it is possible for ordinary people to choose to be extraordinary.”

Relevance of the Elon Musk’s quote in today’s world



A practical way to apply this mindset



Finding out what really matters in a situation

Doing something even if you don't know what will happen

Accepting that not every effort will pay off right away

Learning from results and moving forward





People are often told to think carefully before making decisions in everyday life, especially when they don't know if they will succeed. Most people like situations where the results are clear and safe, whether they are choosing a career path, starting a business, or taking a personal risk. But history and real-life experiences show that many important things have been done when people didn't know what to do. This is where Elon Musk 's quote comes into play. His saying, "You do it even if the odds aren't in your favour," shows a practical way to take action. It changes the focus from chance to goal. The quote says that you should take risks because the importance of a goal can make them worth it. People still talk about this idea when they talk about innovation, leadership, and personal growth in the world today.This quote's main point is that sometimes importance should come before uncertainty. People often hesitate because they don't know what will happen. They might wait for better conditions, more resources, or a better chance of success. But this way of thinking can make you miss out on chances.The quote says that if a goal is really important, you shouldn't let doubt stop you from reaching it. Taking action even when you're not sure what to do doesn't mean ignoring risks.Instead, it means realising that uncertainty is a normal part of doing meaningful work.There are three main parts to this idea:In short, the quote tells people to keep going when something is worth it, even if they aren't sure they will succeed.Many people know Elon Musk because he works for a lot of tech companies. He has been a key figure in companies like Neuralink, SpaceX, and Tesla. Electric cars, space travel, AI, and brain-computer interfaces are some of the things he works on.Musk has worked on projects that were thought to be very risky in the past. For instance:These examples show that what he said is not just a theory. It shows a way of making decisions where long-term importance is more important than short-term certainty.In real life, a lot of decisions are not clear-cut. People don't often have all the facts or a sure thing. But some choices are so important that it might not be possible to wait for certainty.This idea is important in a number of ways:People often pick jobs based on how stable they are instead of how interesting they are. But fields that are important may not always give you security right away. Taking action even when you're not sure can make you happy in the long run.There is a risk involved in starting a business. Uncertainty can come from things like the state of the market, competition, and money problems. But many successful businesses start out without a guarantee of success.It can be risky to choose a field of study based on what you like instead of what is popular. But real interest in the subject is often what leads to long-term growth.In all of these cases, the quote shows that setting meaningful goals might mean putting yourself in risky situations.People often think of risk as something to stay away from, but it can also help things move forward. A lot of changes in science, technology, and society have had results that weren't clear.For example:SpaceX and other companies show how trying and failing over and over can lead to success. Multiple tests led to the development of reusable rockets, which are now a major step forward.This pattern shows that not knowing something isn't always a problem. In many cases, it is part of the process that helps things get better.People often think of the quote when they think of big accomplishments, but it also works for everyday situations. People often have to make choices where the result isn't clear, but the choice is important.Here are some examples:In these cases, the action's value becomes more important than the chance of success.It's important to know that doing something even when you're not sure what will happen doesn't mean you don't care about the results. Making responsible choices still needs careful thought and planning.A balanced approach includes:The quote tells people to do something, but it doesn't tell them to be careless. Instead, it stresses the need to move forward with care and planning.Technology and new ideas are moving quickly in today's world. Artificial intelligence, renewable energy, and space exploration are just a few examples of fields that are always changing. A lot of these changes have uncertain results and require long-term investments.At the same time, people have more options than ever when it comes to their education, jobs, and personal growth. The stress of having to make the "right" choice can make you hesitate.This quote is still relevant because it changes the focus from being sure to being important. It reminds people that not all important actions have clear results.You don't need to make big changes to use this idea. It can start with little things:Over time, this method helps people become more confident and strong.
Article Summary: Quote of the day by elon musk: 'when something is important enough, you do it even if the odds are not in your favour' people often hesitate because they don't know what will happen. The quote says that you should take risks because the importance of a goal can make them worth it.

Article Title: Popular American VC Marc Andreessen to all companies blaming AI for layoffs: You are ‘lying’ as …
Article Text:
Number of open tech roles in 2026 jump about 30%





Popular VC Marc Andreessen has once again stated that “AI job loss” narratives are fake arguing that AI will lead to massive productivity gains which in turn will boost demand and create a large jobs boom. “The "AI job loss" narratives are all fake. AI = massive ramp in productivity = massive ramp in demand = massive jobs boom. Watch,” Andreeseen writes in an X (formerly known as Twitter) post. He quoted a Business Insider article which said that tech openings have rebounded sharply in 2026, “challenging popular narrative that AI is wiping out engineering roles”.“1. Employer recovery from post-COVID hiring correction2. Employer recovery from post-COVID interest rate spike3. Elasticity = demand boom QED,”the VC wrote in another post, challenging interpretations that AI is not displacing engineering jobs, instead framing the increase as QED (quod erat demonstrandum) evidence of cyclical economic recovery rather than proof against AI's labor market effects.The report cited data from TrueUp, a tech hiring analytics firm, which showed more than 67,000 software engineering job openings, the highest level in over three years. As per the data, listings have roughly doubled since a trough in mid-2023."A lot of the 'AI is replacing engineers' narrative isn't grounded in job posting data — at least not so far," Amit Taylor, founder of TrueUp, told the publication.The TrueUp analysis data from late 2022 to 2026. The data showed a steep correction in 2022 and early 2023, when tech companies reduced hiring after over-expanding during the 2020 covid-19 pandemic boom.From mid-2023, hirings began to see an increase, reaching the current 67,000 software engineering job openings in present times.The trend seems contradictory with lesser jobs, especially for recent graduates. Explaining why, Taylor told BI: “Way more people have pursued computer science,” adding “The jobs haven't disappeared, but competition for them is dramatically higher than it was even five years ago.”“Maybe AI compresses some roles entirely. Or maybe it makes great engineers so leveraged that companies fight even harder over them,” Taylor said. “Right now, the demand for top talent is strong, but maybe that continues for a while until things suddenly flip.”
Article Summary: Number of open tech roles in 2026 jump about 30% from mid-2023 to 67,000. Andreessen: ai will lead to massive productivity gains which in turn will boost demand and create a large jobs boom. "a lot of the 'ai is replacing engineers' narrative isn't grounded in job posting data — at least not so far," amit taylor, founder of trueup, told bi.

Article Title: Mustafa Suleyman: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman: For the next couple years at least, entire AI industry is going to be defined by...
Article Text:
Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman argues that the AI industry's future hinges on companies that can afford to run models at scale, not just build the smartest ones. He posits that inference compute scarcity will define winners and losers for the next few years, with high-margin products gaining a significant advantage through a data flywheel of continuous improvement and adoption.

Why inference compute, not AI model training , is the real bottleneck in 2026

How Mustafa Suleyman's AI 'flywheel' gives high-margin products a compounding edge

Watch Microsoft CEO 'Thrilled' About India's Growing Data Centre Capacity, Details Meet With PM Modi Microsoft CEO 'Thrilled' About India's Growing Data Centre Capacity, Details Meet With PM Modi

Consumer AI apps and low-margin AI startups face a token rationing problem

Poll Which type of AI applications do you believe will struggle the most due to token rationing? Consumer AI apps Enterprise solutions Open-source models





Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman says the AI industry's next chapter won't be written by whoever builds the smartest model. It'll be written by whoever can afford to run one at scale. And right now, that's a very short list. In a post on X, Suleyman laid out a sharp, economics-first thesis—arguing that inference compute scarcity, not model intelligence, will define winners and losers for the next two to three years. The companies with the margins to buy tokens pull ahead. Everyone else gets rationed out."For the next couple years at least, the entire AI industry is going to be defined by this fact: demand is going to wildly outstrip supply, and so what matters is which companies / products have margin to pay for tokens," he wrote. The products that can pay, he added, will improve fastest—because lower latency drives retention, retention generates data, and that data spins a flywheel of model improvement and adoption.Suleyman's argument flips the dominant AI narrative. For years, the industry obsessed over training bigger foundation models. But the acute crisis in 2026 is on the serving side—running those models for millions of users in real time.Inference workloads now eat up roughly two-thirds of all AI compute spending, per Deloitte's 2026 TMT Predictions. GPU lead times have stretched to nearly a year. High-bandwidth memory from major suppliers is sold out through 2026. And of the 16 GW of global data-centre capacity slated for this year, only about 5 GW is actually under construction—the rest remains announcements on paper.This scarcity is where Suleyman's flywheel logic takes over. Products with fat gross margins—enterprise legal tools, healthcare SaaS, Microsoft 365 Copilot—can absorb premium inference costs. That buys them lower latency. Lower latency keeps users coming back. Returning users generate rich, proprietary workflow data. That data fine-tunes and improves models. Better models drive more adoption and revenue. Repeat, faster each cycle.Suleyman has used this exact framing before—at the October 2024 IA Summit, he said the winners in vertical AI would be those who "nailed the fine-tuning loop" and got their data flywheel spinning. Microsoft's own numbers back it up: paid Copilot seats hit 15 million in Q2 FY2026, up 160% year-on-year, though still just 3.3% of the 450 million M365 commercial user base.The uncomfortable corollary is that consumer AI apps and cash-strapped startups face a squeeze. Without the margins to buy premium inference, they get slower responses, weaker retention, and a flywheel that never starts spinning.Some in the thread pushed back—arguing intelligence-per-dollar matters more, or that open-source and on-device models could crash inference costs entirely. But Suleyman's bet is clear and well-funded. With Microsoft pouring over $80 billion a year into AI infrastructure, he's banking on the idea that for the next couple of years, the business that can pay for tokens wins the intelligence race first.
Article Summary: Microsoft ai ceo mustafa suleyman argues that the ai industry's future hinges on companies that can afford to run models at scale. He posits that inference compute scarcity will define winners and losers for the next few years. Products with fat gross margins can absorb premium inference costs.

Article Title: Netflix unveils ‘VOID’, an AI model that can change a movie plot
Article Text:
Netflix

How Netflix VOID works







Netflix has unveiled an AI model that can alter the scene. While this is nothing new, Netflix’s model not just remove objects from video, it understands what should happen next, essentially altering the causality. Most object-removal technology leaves a gap where something used to be and/ or fills it with a plausible-looking background. VOID goes a step further: after erasing an object from a scene, it calculates how the surrounding environment should realistically respond to its absence, and in the process it adjusts the behaviour of other elements in the frame accordingly.The model, called VOID, is a vision-language system designed to edit video footage with a level of contextual awareness that sets it apart from several existing tools.For example, imagine this scenario: A movie director has just wrapped up a multi-million dollar action sequence where the star drives straight into a fiery, head-on collision. The cars explode, debris scatters, and the scene is a wrap.But suddenly, there is request to change the scene: The character survives and drive off into the sunset instead. This would mean costly reshoots or millions spent on computer-generated graphics.Netflix’s VOID can alter the scene and digitally erase the collision, the second vehicle, the smoke, and the flying debris. It will then generate new, realistic footage of pristine pavement, making it look as though the main character simply drove down an empty, peaceful road.The AI’s ‘magic’ lies in its grasp of real-world physics. Let us understand this by a different example. If a video shows a person jumping into a pool and splashing water onto the deck, VOID can completely erase the person. It will then intelligently alter the water and the wet concrete, leaving behind a perfectly still, undisturbed pool as if nobody had ever jumped in.“To train the model, we generate a new paired dataset of counterfactual object removals using Kubric and HUMOTO, where removing an object requires altering downstream physical interactions. During inference, a vision-language model identifies regions of the scene affected by the removed object. These regions are then used to guide a video diffusion model that generates physically consistent counterfactual outcomes,” Netflix said.“Experiments on both synthetic and real data show that our approach better preserves consistent scene dynamics after object removal compared to prior video object removal methods,” the company added.Netflix isn’t keeping this powerful tool locked behind studio doors, and made the VOID model available to the public on the AI platform Hugging Face, allowing anyone to download and use it.While there are already several video-altering tools on the market, such as Runway, DiffuEraser, and ProPainter, the Netflix team claims VOID is vastly superior, as per The Register.
Article Summary: Netflix has unveiled an ai model that can alter the scene. While this is nothing new, netflix’s model not just remove objects from video, it understands what should happen next. Most object-removal technology leaves a gap where something used to be and/ or fills it with a plausible-looking background.

Article Title: AI data centers are causing 'stress' not just to tech companies, but also private insurers
Article Text:
Massive investments, growing risks



Unique challenges for data centre insurance companies





AI data centers are reportedly putting new pressure on the insurance industry. According to a CNBC report, experts warn that the rapid growth of these projects is becoming a “stress test” not just for tech companies, but also for private insurers and lenders. Consulting firm McKinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. As costs rise, tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects. The scale of investments is creating challenges for insurers.“When you put $10 to $20 billion plus in a single location, it creates capacity issues in the marketplace,” said Tom Harper, data center leader at insurance broker Gallagher as quoted in the CNBC report.He added that the sector has been a “real stress test” over the last few years, as insurers struggle to provide coverage for such large and complex projects. While these facilities are seen as high-quality assets, Harper said insuring them has become difficult due to their size and risk concentration.Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. Companies including Nvidia, Microsoft and BlackRock have already taken part in large investment deals.Rajat Rana, partner at Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan, described the scale as unprecedented. “We’re talking about trillions of dollars, and almost going back to the same cycle where there’s almost no transparency about the financing structures — the scale is astronomical,” he said. He added that the boom in AI data centers could be “the largest peacetime investment project in human history.”Experts say the way these projects are financed is adding another layer of risk. Much of the funding is happening through complex and less transparent debt structures. Rana warned that this trend feels like “deja vu,” referring to past financial cycles. He said the lack of transparency could create risks for investors, insurers and pension funds.As stated in the CNBC report, data centers offer unique challenges due to their mix of real estate and advanced technology. Insurers are now designing custom policies to manage these risks. However, challenges remain. Large facilities are often located in areas prone to extreme weather, and they store expensive equipment, sometimes even before installation.Another key concern is the mismatch between the lifespan of data centers and the hardware they use. While data centers can last decades, GPUs — the chips powering AI — typically last around seven years. Rana described this as a “GPU debt treadmill,” saying, “This is almost like a treadmill that these AI data centers are running on.”He explained that companies may need to keep raising debt to upgrade infrastructure as technology evolves.
Article Summary: Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. Mckinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. Tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects.

Article Title: Clavicular Viral Controversy: "Trans women are.....": Clavicular’s viral moment with trans women sparks fresh conversation on internet culture
Article Text:
A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Instead of conflict, the exchange highlights shared influence and mutual awareness. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Clavicular and trans women share a surprising realization about online trends







The video of Clavicular, a short-video producer, is going around the Internet, yet not the reason why. There is no yelling or over-the-top drama. It is a mere, everyday talk that seems almost novel nowadays, in the age of internet, which is overflowing with content. The scene is easy-going, the tone of the narrative is straightforward, and the characters appear more concerned with conversation than combat.The message that is being addressed is the flow of the conversation. It discusses internet culture, the way people communicate with one another and the place of trends. Rather than bringing out a debate, the encounter towards curiosity. It quietly brings attention to something that a lot of people who use the internet notice but don't often say out loud. People in different communities often have a bigger impact on each other than they think.The conversation begins with a confident and lighthearted comment from one of the trans women present. She says, "You know that trans women are like the OG lip masters, right? We are very talented in the program. Like, I do the peptides, I'm on Zeta." Her tone carries a mix of pride and humor, setting a relaxed vibe at the table.Clavicular responds in a way that keeps the mood steady but adds a different angle.He says, "That is funny because you guys were using a lot of the words in like the South Korean subreddits in like 2014." It’s not confrontational. If anything, it feels like an observation. He points to how certain words and ideas may have traveled across different online spaces over time.Another voice joins in and brings the moment full circle. "Guys, we're like the OGs. I saw a couple of posts about that. We're like copying each other." That line hits in a simple but honest way. It reflects something familiar. Online culture is rarely created in isolation. It grows through sharing, borrowing, and sometimes even unknowingly repeating.That's what makes the clip so powerful. It doesn't want to make a point or win an argument. It only shows a group of people talking, listening, and figuring something out together.This moment feels different in a space where viral content often leads to fights. It reminds people that conversations can be calm, thoughtful, and still important. And sometimes, that honest silence is what makes it work.
Article Summary: A viral clip featuring clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Article Title: 6Ix9Ine Streaming: Rapper 6ix9ine officially retires from music, shifts focus to streaming
Article Text:
Image Via Instagram

“washed up.”

“I just know what I’m good at,”

6ix9ine starts a new chapter by focusing only on streaming

“I don’t think the music industry is washed up.

If he’s tired of singing, let him just say so.”

“I don’t blame him. He’s been carrying rap since 2018.”







Rapper 6ix9ine, who is also known as Daniel Hernandez, has surprised his fans with a shocking decision. The artist, in a stream hosted by popular streamer Adin Ross, has announced that he is retiring from the music industry since it isHe added,showing that he wants to focus on something he enjoys and can succeed in. Fans who have followed his music career were surprised by this sudden change.This was 6ix9ine’s first stream after getting out of jail. During the stream, Adin Ross asked him if he would drop any new music. 6ix9ine clearly said no and explained that he is now going to focus fully on streaming. The moment, which happened in the livestream, quickly went viral. It marks a big change for the rapper, who is known for his loud personality and colorful music career.With the shift to streaming, 6ix9ine will spend more time on the Internet, engaging in activities directly with his fans. He will no longer have to record songs but instead share himself with the world through live videos where people can interact and see him at their convenience. This shift grants him more control of his career and fan base.He appeared to be happy in the stream and was excited about his future in streaming, indicating his contentment with his life choices.His transition into streaming has received mixed reactions from fans. Many appreciate that he is making an effort to branch out into other ventures, while others have expressed disappointment over his departure from music.One user said,Another said,In the end, it’s a landmark decision for 6ix9ine, as he is retiring from being a musician. While this may seem like bad news for the music industry, streaming sites will gain another performer who knows how to entertain people. The spotlight will now be on him, with everyone waiting to see how famous he becomes.
Article Summary: Rapper 6ix9ine, also known as daniel hernandez, has surprised his fans with a shocking decision. The artist, in a stream hosted by popular streamer adin ross, has announced that he is retiring from the music industry. The moment, which happened in the livestream, quickly went viral.

Article Title: H-1B Visa Alternatives: Not end of road for those who didn't get selected in H-1B lottery: Immigration attorney explains 8 best options
Article Text:
There are at least 8 best options for those who did not get selected in the H-1B FY2027 lottery, immigration attorney Felipe Jimenez said.



Cap-exempt H-1B

Change to F-1 visa

F-1 STEM OPT extension

Day 1 CPT program

O-1 visa

L-1 visa

TN visa for Canadians and Mexicans

E-2 visa







Not getting picked up in the H-1B lottery that has now concluded for FY 2027 is not the end of the road for American Dream, immigration attorney Felipe Jimenez said, as there are many other visa programs and viable options to remain legally in the US. Several Redditors expressed their frustration as they said this was their last chance to stay in the US. Jimenez said there are at least eight best options and not only the much-discused O-visa. O-visa has come on the radar as questions are being raised as to whether people with indeed extraordinary abilities are getting that visa or whether it has become an alternative to H-1B visa.Some experts suggested that there is a possibility that a second lottery may take place as they estimate that companies may not file petitions for all those who got selected in the lottery as the companies will have to pay $100,000 visa fee. And so, a second lottery may be required to fill all the cap spots.The just-concluded lottery is for the H-1B cap but there are cap-exempt H-1b jobs as well. Universities and higher education institutions, nonprofit entities affiliated with universities, nonprofit research organizations, and government research organizations are not subject to this 85,000 cap.Returning to school is a viable option to remain legally in the US as this changes the visa program again to F-1 and gives extra time, making the individual eligible for OPT, OPT extension.STEM students on F-1 visa who are already doing their OPT can qualify for a 24-month STEM OPT extension.A Day 1 CPT program is a type of academic program in the US that allows international students (usually on an F-1 visa) to start working immediately from the first day of their course through something called Curricular Practical Training (CPT).Jimenez advised to use this program with caution as this can lead to future visa denials if the USCIS determines the program is not legitimate.This visa program is for people with extraordinary ability in sciemce, education, business, athletics, arts, film and television. One has to prove the extraordinary ability through publications, awards, high salary etc.Those who work for a multinational company, they can get L-1 visa for intracompany transfer.The TN (Trade NAFTA) visa allows Canadian and Mexican citizens to work in the United States in certain professional occupations. This is faster and simpler and has no lottery and Canadians can apply for it directly at the border. This visa, however, does not lead to a Green Card.This is for entrepreneurs who can invest substantially in a US business. Individuals who have access to funds can take this route. The USCIS will scrutinize the souce of funds.
Article Summary: There are at least eight best options for those who did not get selected in the h-1b fy2027 lottery, immigration attorney felipe jimenez said. O-visa has come on the radar as questions are being raised as to whether people with indeed extraordinary abilities are getting that visa.

Article Title: H1B Visa: 7 H-1B workers inside a mailbox: US reporter claims ‘fraud’ involving software engineers in Texas
Article Text:
Watch Trump's H-1B Visa Chaos: Jobs At Risk, Families Traumatised As Wait Pushed To Late 2026 Trump's H-1B Visa Chaos: Jobs At Risk, Families Traumatised As Wait Pushed To Late 2026







Seven H-1B workers inside a mailbox. That is the striking claim made by a US social media influencer after an on-ground visit to a Texas company raised questions about possible visa fraud.A user known as “Based Jessica” posted on X alleging "irregularities" involving Innovative Data Analytics Group LLC in Plano, Texas. According to her post, seven H-1B visa workers were approved to work at the firm, which is listed as employing software engineers. However, she claimed the company had no website and was stationed in a small mailbox rather than a functioning office of 7 people.In a video shared online, BlazeTV's conservative political commentator Sara Gonzales is seen attempting to locate the business at its registered address. When she checks Suite 78, she finds only a mailbox instead of an active workplace. After the discovery, she went straight to the company owner to ask where the listed employees are and if they are genuinely working at the site.The reporter visits the residential address of the company’s owner, Uma Devi. During the interaction, Devi confirms that the company is in her name but struggles to provide clarity about its operations or the employees’ whereabouts.When asked about the workers, Devi says they are at the “office” but admits she does not know the “exact address”. After being told that suite 78 had already been checked, the reporter confronts Devi, asking if the workers are inside the mailbox. Devi responds, “No" and adds that she would need to check with her husband.After the exchange, the reporter concluded that the business owner appears to have 'no idea' about her own company. The video and its claims have made some users question a potential H-1B visa fraud operation in Texas.
Article Summary: 'based jessica' posted on x alleging 'irregularities' involving innovative data analytics group llc in plano, texas. According to her post, seven h-1b visa workers were approved to work at the firm. She claimed the company had no website and was stationed in a small mailbox rather than a functioning office of 7 people.

Article Title: Indian man appears in court charged with mowing down pedestrians in Derby
Article Text:
Sandhu Ponnachan



TOI Correspondent from London:







An Indian man, believed to be from Kerala, has been charged with driving a car into pedestrians in Derby on Saturday night, leaving seven people seriously injured.Sandhu Ponnachan (36), who moved to Derby in April 2025 and is an Indian citizen, appeared at Southern Derbyshire magistrates’ court on Wednesday where he was charged with six counts of grievous bodily harm (GBH) with intent, one count of attempted GBH with intent, one count of dangerous driving and one count of possession of a bladed article. He spoke only to confirm his name and address and did not enter a plea. He was remanded in custody to appear at Derby crown court on April 29. There was no application for bail.Four men and three women, aged 36 to 52, had to be hospitalised after a black car mounted the pavement and ploughed into pedestrians at 9.30pm local time on March 28. Ponnachan was arrested on suspicion of attempted murder shortly afterwards in a visibly damaged car. Four people have since been discharged from hospital.His LinkedIn profile shows he was looking for work as a logistics manager, having worked in this role for Caterpillar Inc until March 2026.He was qualified to operate a forklift and work as a security guard. He is also listed as a director of Greeshmam Ltd.On Facebook Ponnachan has put up posts praising India’s rise as a great power and a video asking “Why did Kerala go bankrupt?” as well as posts in Malayalam. There are religious posts, including a photo showing Verse 10 of Śrī Rūpa Gosvāmī’s Upadeśāmṛta (Nectar of Instruction); a video of a painting of Lord Hanuman and of the Bhagavad Gita, and a repost of a post by Congress politician Shashi Tharoor showing the Ram Lalla idol at Ram Mandir. One repost blames the Congress-led UPA regime for the “mysterious deaths of India’s nuclear scientists”.The revelation of his nationality has triggered a barrage of racist abuse at Indians on social media, leading to the Friends of India Society International UK issuing a statement urging people not to let the actions of one person stigmatise an entire community. Users wrote on X: “Mass Immigration has been a disaster for this country. We need deportations.” Another wrote: “Annexing India and restoring the Raj will protect the world against India. ” Another wrote: “You shouldn’t be surprised. Look what Indians are doing to Canada. They are a crime syndicate.”
Article Summary: Sandhu ponnachan (36) moved to derby in april 2025 and is an indian citizen. He appeared at southern derbyshire magistrates’ court on wednesday. He was remanded in custody to appear at derby crown court on april 29.

Article Title: Sweet heist: 12 tonnes of KitKat stolen in Europe ahead of Easter
Article Text:
Representative image (AI-generated)





Swiss food brand Nestle has reported the theft of a large consignment of its popular KitKat bars in Europe, warning that the incident could disrupt supplies ahead of the Easter season.In a statement, the company confirmed that a truck carrying 413,793 units of its newly launched chocolate range was stolen while in transit.The shipment, weighing around 12 tonnes, went missing last week during transportation between production and distribution points, news agency AFP reported.According to the company, the truck had departed from central Italy and was en route to Poland, with plans to distribute the products across multiple European countries along the way.However, the exact location where the theft occurred has not been disclosed and both the vehicle and its cargo remain untraceable.A spokesperson for the brand, referring to its well-known tagline, said the company has always encouraged consumers to “have a break” with KitKat, but added that in this case, “thieves appear to have taken the message too literally.”"We've always encouraged people to have a break with KitKat.But it seems thieves have taken the message too literally and made a break with more than 12 tonnes of our chocolate," the spokesperson said.Nestle warned that the theft could lead to temporary shortages of KitKat bars in stores, particularly at a time when demand typically rises ahead of Easter.The company also cautioned that the stolen products may enter unofficial or unauthorised sales channels across European markets.Investigations into the incident are currently underway in coordination with local authorities and supply chain partners.Nestlé said it is working to trace the missing consignment using unique batch codes printed on each bar. If identified, the system will prompt users with instructions to alert the company, enabling further action."If a match is found, the scanner will be given clear instructions on how to alert KitKat who will then share the evidence appropriately," the company said.The brand has urged retailers and consumers to remain cautious, as efforts continue to recover the stolen shipment and prevent its circulation in the market.
Article Summary: Truck carrying 413,793 units of nestle's newly launched chocolate range was stolen while in transit. The shipment, weighing around 12 tonnes, went missing last week. Nestle warned that the theft could lead to temporary shortages of kitkat bars in stores.

Article Title: UAE extends Nafis programme to 2040; 176,000 Emiratis employed so far
Article Text:
UAE extends Nafis programme to 2040.

What is the Nafis programme?





Why it matters











The UAE has extended its flagship Nafis programme until 2040, signalling a long-term push to strengthen Emiratisation and expand opportunities for national talent in the private sector.The announcement was made by Sheikh Mansour bin Zayed Al Nahyan, Vice President, Deputy Prime Minister, and Chairman of the Presidential Court. The extension comes under the directives of UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, reinforcing the country’s commitment to building a sustainable national workforce.Alongside the extension, authorities introduced a series of new initiatives focused on social and workforce support. These include a programme designed to support the children of Emirati mothers working in the private sector, as well as a separate initiative aimed at supporting the wives of Emirati citizens employed in private companies.The children’s allowance under Nafis has also been expanded, removing the cap on the number of children covered. This change is expected to strengthen family stability while encouraging greater participation of Emiratis in private sector roles.The programme has already seen significant uptake. So far, 176,000 Emiratis have been employed through Nafis, with 152,000 currently working in the private sector. Women account for 74 per cent of beneficiaries, while 38,000 individuals have received support through the children’s allowance programme.Nafis has also driven strong engagement from employers, with around 32,000 companies now employing Emirati talent under the initiative, reflecting a growing shift towards private sector participation.Nafis is a federal initiative launched by the UAE to boost the competitiveness of Emirati nationals and increase their participation in the private sector. The programme aims to enable 75,000 Emiratis to take up private sector jobs by 2025.It requires companies with more than 50 employees to meet specific hiring quotas for Emiratis. To support this, Nafis offers salary subsidies, training programmes, and financial incentives, helping nationals transition into and sustain private sector employment.The extension of Nafis to 2040 reflects the UAE’s long-term strategy to reduce reliance on public sector employment and strengthen the role of citizens in the private sector.By expanding benefits such as family support and removing limits on children’s allowances, the programme also addresses key social factors that influence career choices, making private sector roles more viable for Emiratis.At a broader level, Nafis is designed to build a more balanced and sustainable labour market, where national talent plays a larger role in driving economic growth while aligning workforce development with the country’s future economic priorities.
Article Summary: Uae extends nafis programme to 2040. The extension comes under the directives of uae president sheikh mohamed bin zayed al nahyan. The programme aims to enable 75,000 emiratis to take up private sector jobs by 2025. It requires companies with more than 50 employees to meet hiring quotas for emiratis.
